meta.title: GCP-PDE-2: Architecture, BigQuery and Dataflow Operations

domains.name: Preparing and Using Data for Analysis

questions.stem: Your company processes IoT sensor data arriving at 50,000 events per second. The data must be processed in real-time for anomaly detection and also stored for historical analysis. Which Google Cloud architecture best satisfies both requirements?

options.text: Publish events to Pub/Sub, use Dataflow for streaming processing and anomaly detection, write results to BigQuery for historical analysis
Stream events directly into BigQuery using the streaming insert API, then query with scheduled jobs for anomaly detection
Ingest events into Cloud Storage, use Cloud Composer to trigger Dataproc batch jobs for anomaly detection
Use Cloud Spanner as the primary ingestion layer to handle the write throughput, then export to BigQuery nightly

questions.explanation:
Pub/Sub decouples ingestion from processing and handles bursty high-throughput workloads. Dataflow (Apache Beam) provides a unified streaming/batch model for real-time anomaly detection with exactly-once semantics. BigQuery accepts Dataflow output for durable, low-cost historical analysis. This lambda/kappa-style architecture is the canonical GCP pattern for this use case.

[Professional Data Engineer exam guide](https://cloud.google.com/learn/certification/data-engineer)
questions.reference: Google Cloud Professional Data Engineer exam guide and product docs

questions.stem: A financial services company needs a database solution that supports multi-region strong consistency, ACID transactions across rows and tables, and SQL querying. Which Google Cloud product should be used?

options.text: Cloud Bigtable
Cloud Spanner
Firestore in Datastore mode
Cloud SQL with read replicas

questions.explanation:
Cloud Spanner is the only Google Cloud database that provides globally distributed, horizontally scalable, strongly consistent, ACID-transactional relational storage with standard SQL support. Cloud Bigtable is NoSQL (no transactions). Cloud SQL does not offer multi-region strong consistency. Firestore is NoSQL with limited transaction scope.

[Professional Data Engineer exam guide](https://cloud.google.com/learn/certification/data-engineer)
questions.reference: Google Cloud Professional Data Engineer exam guide and product docs

questions.stem: You are designing a data lake on Google Cloud. Raw files of varying formats (JSON, CSV, Avro, Parquet) arrive daily. You need cost-effective storage with the ability to run ad-hoc queries without loading data first. Which approach is best?

options.text: Store files in Cloud Storage and use BigQuery external tables to query them in place
Load all files into Cloud Bigtable and query using HBase API
Convert all files to CSV and load into Cloud SQL
Use Dataflow to pre-process files and load into Firestore before querying

questions.explanation:
BigQuery external tables (formerly federated queries) allow querying data directly in Cloud Storage in formats including CSV, JSON, Avro, Parquet, and ORC without ingestion costs. Cloud Storage provides durable, low-cost object storage ideal for a data lake landing zone. This avoids ETL before exploration.

[Professional Data Engineer exam guide](https://cloud.google.com/learn/certification/data-engineer)
questions.reference: Google Cloud Professional Data Engineer exam guide and product docs

questions.stem: A Dataflow streaming pipeline is experiencing high watermark lag. The pipeline reads from Pub/Sub, performs a windowed aggregation with a 5-minute fixed window, and writes to BigQuery. What is the most likely cause and remediation?

options.text: The BigQuery write sink is the bottleneck; switch from streaming inserts to Storage Write API batch mode
Late-arriving data is holding the watermark back; set an allowed lateness and trigger early firings with accumulatingFiredPanes mode
The Pub/Sub subscription has too many undelivered messages; increase Dataflow worker count and set maxNumWorkers
Windowed aggregation is not supported in streaming mode; switch to Dataproc Spark Structured Streaming

questions.explanation:
Watermark lag in windowed Dataflow pipelines is typically caused by late data. The pipeline waits for out-of-order events before emitting the window result, which holds the watermark. Setting allowedLateness with ACCUMULATING_FIRED_PANES allows the pipeline to emit early results and update on late arrivals, keeping the watermark advancing. Increasing workers addresses throughput but not watermark advancement.

[Professional Data Engineer exam guide](https://cloud.google.com/learn/certification/data-engineer)
questions.reference: Google Cloud Professional Data Engineer exam guide and product docs

questions.stem: You need to migrate 200 TB of on-premises relational data to BigQuery with minimal downtime. The data resides in PostgreSQL. Which Google Cloud service is most appropriate for the initial bulk load?

options.text: Database Migration Service (DMS)
Datastream with continuous replication
Transfer Appliance for physical shipment
BigQuery Data Transfer Service

questions.explanation:
Database Migration Service (DMS) is designed for migrating relational databases (MySQL, PostgreSQL, SQL Server, Oracle) to fully managed Cloud SQL or AlloyDB destinations, and can also export to BigQuery. For 200 TB over the network with minimal downtime, DMS provides continuous replication (CDC) to keep the target in sync while the bulk load occurs, cutting over with minimal downtime. Transfer Appliance is for offline data transfer, not database migrations.

[Professional Data Engineer exam guide](https://cloud.google.com/learn/certification/data-engineer)
questions.reference: Google Cloud Professional Data Engineer exam guide and product docs

questions.stem: A Dataflow batch pipeline processes files from Cloud Storage and writes results to BigQuery. Jobs are failing intermittently with OutOfMemoryError on workers. The pipeline performs a large CoGroupByKey operation. What should you do?

options.text: Increase the number of workers using --numWorkers flag
Enable Dataflow Shuffle service to offload shuffle data off worker memory
Rewrite the pipeline to use Cloud Dataproc instead
Increase worker disk size using --diskSizeGb flag

questions.explanation:
Dataflow Shuffle service (--experiments=shuffle_mode=service) offloads the shuffle phase to a managed backend, removing intermediate shuffle data from worker memory and disk. This is the recommended solution for OOM errors caused by large GroupByKey or CoGroupByKey operations. Increasing workers distributes load but does not reduce per-key memory requirements if keys are skewed.

[Professional Data Engineer exam guide](https://cloud.google.com/learn/certification/data-engineer)
questions.reference: Google Cloud Professional Data Engineer exam guide and product docs

questions.stem: You need to run a recurring ETL pipeline that extracts data from Cloud Storage, transforms it using PySpark, and loads it into BigQuery. The job runs once daily and must minimize cost. Which service should you use?

options.text: Dataproc on a persistent cluster with scheduled jobs
Dataproc with ephemeral cluster creation per job run, using preemptible VMs
Dataflow with a batch pipeline triggered by Cloud Scheduler
Cloud Run jobs executing a PySpark container

questions.explanation:
Dataproc with ephemeral clusters (create at job start, delete at completion) minimizes cost by charging only for active job runtime. Using preemptible (spot) VMs for worker nodes further reduces cost by up to 80%. A persistent cluster incurs idle costs. Dataflow is also valid but Dataproc is preferred for existing PySpark code without rewrite to Beam.

[Professional Data Engineer exam guide](https://cloud.google.com/learn/certification/data-engineer)
questions.reference: Google Cloud Professional Data Engineer exam guide and product docs

questions.stem: Your team uses Apache Kafka on-premises for event streaming. You are migrating to Google Cloud. Which two Google Cloud services can act as a managed replacement for Kafka topics with minimal application code changes? (Select TWO)

options.text: Cloud Pub/Sub
Cloud Pub/Sub Lite
Cloud Bigtable
Managed Kafka on Google Cloud (Confluent)

questions.explanation:
Cloud Pub/Sub is GCP's fully managed message broker with Kafka-compatible semantics (at-least-once delivery, fan-out). Managed Service for Apache Kafka on Google Cloud (offered via Confluent or native managed Kafka) provides native Kafka API compatibility, allowing existing producers/consumers to connect with no code changes. Pub/Sub Lite is a lower-cost variant with zonal availability. Bigtable is a NoSQL database, not a message queue.

[Professional Data Engineer exam guide](https://cloud.google.com/learn/certification/data-engineer)
questions.reference: Google Cloud Professional Data Engineer exam guide and product docs

questions.stem: A Dataflow streaming pipeline uses session windows to group user events. Sessions close after 30 minutes of user inactivity. You notice some sessions never close and the pipeline accumulates unbounded state. What is the root cause?

options.text: Session windows are not supported in streaming mode; use sliding windows instead
A small subset of users generates a continuous stream of events with no gaps, preventing their session window from ever closing
The Pub/Sub subscription is not acknowledging messages, causing re-delivery that resets window timers
Missing allowedLateness configuration means Beam never garbage-collects closed window state

questions.explanation:
Session windows close only when no events arrive within the gap duration (30 minutes here). If certain users generate continuous high-frequency events, their session window grows indefinitely, accumulating state. This is a fundamental property of session windowing, not a bug. Solutions include capping session duration with a maxSessionDuration or switching to fixed/sliding windows for such users.

[Professional Data Engineer exam guide](https://cloud.google.com/learn/certification/data-engineer)
questions.reference: Google Cloud Professional Data Engineer exam guide and product docs

questions.stem: You want to schedule a Cloud Composer (Apache Airflow) DAG to run every day at 6 AM UTC and send an alert email if any task fails. Which Airflow feature handles the failure notification?

options.text: SLA miss callbacks at the DAG level
The on_failure_callback parameter on tasks or email_on_failure=True with email configuration
Cloud Monitoring alerting policies triggered by Airflow log entries
Pub/Sub topic subscription bound to the Airflow task_failed event

questions.explanation:
Airflow's on_failure_callback or email_on_failure=True with SMTP configuration is the built-in mechanism for task failure notifications. Setting email_on_failure=True and providing email addresses in the default_args or task arguments causes Airflow to send an alert automatically. Cloud Monitoring can also alert on logs but requires additional setup and is not the idiomatic Airflow approach.

[Professional Data Engineer exam guide](https://cloud.google.com/learn/certification/data-engineer)
questions.reference: Google Cloud Professional Data Engineer exam guide and product docs

questions.stem: You need to store time-series metrics from 10,000 IoT devices, with each device writing one record per second. Queries access data for a single device over a time range. Which storage system is optimal?

options.text: Cloud Spanner
Cloud Bigtable
Cloud Firestore
Cloud SQL

questions.explanation:
Cloud Bigtable is purpose-built for time-series workloads: it handles millions of reads/writes per second, stores data in lexicographically sorted rows, and supports range scans over row keys. Designing the row key as deviceId#timestamp provides O(1) device lookups and efficient time-range scans. Cloud Spanner and Cloud SQL support fewer writes per second at this scale, and Firestore is document-oriented and not optimized for time-series range queries.

[Professional Data Engineer exam guide](https://cloud.google.com/learn/certification/data-engineer)
questions.reference: Google Cloud Professional Data Engineer exam guide and product docs

questions.stem: A BigQuery table is partitioned by ingestion date. Queries that filter on a specific date column (event_date) are not pruning partitions and scan the full table. What is the most likely cause?

options.text: BigQuery only prunes partitions when the filter uses _PARTITIONDATE or _PARTITIONTIME pseudo-columns
The table uses ingestion-time partitioning but the query filters on event_date, a different column; partitions are pruned only on the partition column
The partition expiration is set too low, causing BigQuery to bypass pruning
Queries must use TABLESAMPLE to enable partition pruning

questions.explanation:
Ingestion-time partitioned tables use _PARTITIONTIME (the load timestamp) as the partition key, not any column in the data. Filtering on event_date (a data column) does not prune partitions because it is a different field. To prune on event_date, the table should be converted to column-based partitioning on event_date using a DATE or TIMESTAMP column.

[Professional Data Engineer exam guide](https://cloud.google.com/learn/certification/data-engineer)
questions.reference: Google Cloud Professional Data Engineer exam guide and product docs

questions.stem: You need to choose between Cloud Storage storage classes for the following data: (1) ML training datasets accessed weekly, (2) compliance archives accessed once per year. Which storage classes minimize cost respectively?

options.text: Standard for training datasets; Coldline for compliance archives
Nearline for training datasets; Archive for compliance archives
Standard for both
Nearline for training datasets; Coldline for compliance archives

questions.explanation:
Nearline is optimized for data accessed less than once per month (weekly access means ~4x/month — Standard may be cheaper here, but Nearline is correct for data accessed about once per month). Archive class (formerly Coldline Archive) is for data accessed less than once per year with the lowest storage cost. For compliance archives accessed once per year, Archive is the correct choice. Coldline is for data accessed < once per quarter.

[Professional Data Engineer exam guide](https://cloud.google.com/learn/certification/data-engineer)
questions.reference: Google Cloud Professional Data Engineer exam guide and product docs

questions.stem: A BigQuery dataset contains a table with 500 billion rows growing by 50 million rows per day. Query performance on recent data has degraded. The table is clustered on user_id and partitioned by date. Analysts primarily query the last 30 days, filtered by user_id. What should you do first?

options.text: Add a second clustering column for event_type to narrow scans further
Enable BigQuery BI Engine to cache frequently accessed partitions
Check clustering effectiveness with INFORMATION_SCHEMA.TABLE_STORAGE; if blocks are fragmented, run a manual CLUSTER BY re-clustering job
Migrate the table to Cloud Bigtable for better range scan performance

questions.explanation:
BigQuery automatically re-clusters tables, but on very large tables with high write rates, clustering can become fragmented. Checking INFORMATION_SCHEMA.TABLE_STORAGE reveals block metrics. For heavily fragmented tables, a SELECT * except() INTO OVERWRITE (or CREATE TABLE AS SELECT with CLUSTER BY) re-clusters the data. This is the diagnostic-first approach before adding complexity. Bigtable migration is unnecessary for analytical workloads.

[Professional Data Engineer exam guide](https://cloud.google.com/learn/certification/data-engineer)
questions.reference: Google Cloud Professional Data Engineer exam guide and product docs

questions.stem: You need to store semi-structured JSON documents for a user profile service. Profiles are queried by user_id and sub-fields are accessed in application code. The workload is 90% reads. Which Google Cloud database is most suitable?

options.text: Cloud Bigtable
Cloud Firestore (Native mode)
Cloud Spanner with JSON column type
BigQuery with JSON column type

questions.explanation:
Firestore in Native mode is a fully managed NoSQL document database designed exactly for semi-structured document storage. It supports real-time updates, sub-collection queries, strong consistency for single-document reads, and scales to millions of users. It is optimized for high-read transactional workloads typical of user profile services. BigQuery is OLAP and not suitable for low-latency user profile lookups.

[Professional Data Engineer exam guide](https://cloud.google.com/learn/certification/data-engineer)
questions.reference: Google Cloud Professional Data Engineer exam guide and product docs

questions.stem: You need to build an interactive dashboard with sub-second query response times on a BigQuery table with 10 billion rows. Users filter by region and date range. Which feature should you enable?

options.text: BigQuery Reservations (slots)
BigQuery BI Engine
Materialized views with auto-refresh
Connected Sheets

questions.explanation:
BigQuery BI Engine is an in-memory analysis service that caches hot data for sub-second query response times, designed specifically for dashboard workloads. It integrates transparently with Looker Studio, Looker, and direct BigQuery queries. Materialized views help but do not guarantee sub-second latency for arbitrary filters. Reservations improve throughput but not individual query latency.

[Professional Data Engineer exam guide](https://cloud.google.com/learn/certification/data-engineer)
questions.reference: Google Cloud Professional Data Engineer exam guide and product docs

questions.stem: A data analyst wants to run SQL queries on a BigQuery table but should not be able to see the credit_card_number or ssn columns. What is the recommended approach using native BigQuery features?

options.text: Create an authorized view that excludes sensitive columns and grant the analyst access to the view, not the base table
Use column-level security with policy tags and assign the analyst a role without the associated data policy binding
Encrypt the sensitive columns with Cloud KMS and do not provide the analyst with decryption keys
Delete the sensitive columns and recreate them in a separate restricted table

questions.explanation:
BigQuery column-level security uses policy tags (defined in Data Catalog) applied to sensitive columns. Access to tagged columns is controlled by IAM bindings on the policy tag. Users without the Fine-Grained Reader role on the policy tag receive an error when querying those columns. This is the native, scalable approach. Authorized views also work but require maintenance as schema evolves.

[Professional Data Engineer exam guide](https://cloud.google.com/learn/certification/data-engineer)
questions.reference: Google Cloud Professional Data Engineer exam guide and product docs

questions.stem: You are building a feature engineering pipeline for a machine learning model. Raw features include numeric values, categorical strings, and timestamp fields. Which Google Cloud service provides managed feature transformation and storage for training and serving consistency?

options.text: Vertex AI Feature Store
Cloud Dataprep
Dataflow with TensorFlow Transform
BigQuery ML feature preprocessing

questions.explanation:
Vertex AI Feature Store provides a centralized repository for storing, sharing, and serving ML features. It ensures training-serving skew elimination by using the same feature transformations and serving the same feature values at training time (offline serving from BigQuery) and inference time (online serving via low-latency API). TFTransform and Dataflow are valid for transform computation but do not provide a feature serving layer.

[Professional Data Engineer exam guide](https://cloud.google.com/learn/certification/data-engineer)
questions.reference: Google Cloud Professional Data Engineer exam guide and product docs

questions.stem: A BigQuery query joins two large tables (10 billion rows and 500 million rows) and takes 45 minutes. EXPLAIN plan shows a broadcast join. The smaller table is 8 GB. What should you do to improve performance?

options.text: Partition both tables on the join key column to enable partition pruning during the join
Force a shuffle join by setting the join hint to HASH; BigQuery broadcast joins are always slower
The 8 GB table exceeds the broadcast join threshold; BigQuery should have chosen shuffle; investigate whether query cache is stale or statistics are outdated
Pre-aggregate the 500M row table into a materialized view and join against the view

questions.explanation:
When a join involves a very large table (500M rows) that cannot be broadcast, pre-aggregating it in a materialized view reduces its size significantly before the join. This is the most effective optimization for this scenario. Partitioning helps with pruning but does not reduce join cardinality. BigQuery's broadcast join threshold is ~1 GB, so at 8 GB it already performs a shuffle join — the EXPLAIN showing a broadcast join is unusual and may indicate different statistics.

[Professional Data Engineer exam guide](https://cloud.google.com/learn/certification/data-engineer)
questions.reference: Google Cloud Professional Data Engineer exam guide and product docs

questions.stem: You want to use Vertex AI AutoML to build a tabular classification model. Your dataset has 100,000 rows and 50 features. Three features contain 40% null values. What should you do before training?

options.text: Remove the three features with missing values; AutoML cannot handle nulls
AutoML Tables handles missing values automatically; review feature importance after training to decide whether to impute or drop
Impute missing values with the column mean before uploading the dataset
Increase the dataset to at least 1 million rows before training; 100K is too small for AutoML

questions.explanation:
Vertex AI AutoML for tabular data handles missing values natively as part of its automated feature engineering. It can learn from the absence pattern itself. The best practice is to allow AutoML to train first, then review the feature importance report. If features with high nullity have low importance, they can be dropped. 100K rows is sufficient for AutoML (minimum is 1,000 rows).

[Professional Data Engineer exam guide](https://cloud.google.com/learn/certification/data-engineer)
questions.reference: Google Cloud Professional Data Engineer exam guide and product docs

questions.stem: A Dataflow streaming job must be updated to add new processing logic without losing any in-flight Pub/Sub messages or state. What is the correct procedure?

options.text: Cancel the job, update the pipeline code, and start a new job from the latest Pub/Sub snapshot
Use Dataflow job update (--update flag) with a compatible pipeline graph to replace the job in-place
Deploy the new code as a separate job, drain the old job, then redirect Pub/Sub traffic to the new subscription
Pause the job with the drain command, update the template, and resume

questions.explanation:
Dataflow supports in-place streaming job updates via the --update flag, provided the new pipeline graph is compatible with the running graph (same transform names and step types). The update preserves in-flight state and does not require a Pub/Sub snapshot or creating a new subscription. Cancelling drops messages. Draining + new job works but requires subscription management. The --update approach is the recommended zero-downtime method.

[Professional Data Engineer exam guide](https://cloud.google.com/learn/certification/data-engineer)
questions.reference: Google Cloud Professional Data Engineer exam guide and product docs

questions.stem: You need to monitor BigQuery slot utilization across projects in your organization and alert when utilization exceeds 80% for more than 5 minutes. Which approach should you use?

options.text: Query INFORMATION_SCHEMA.JOBS_BY_ORGANIZATION every minute from a Cloud Scheduler + Cloud Functions job
Use Cloud Monitoring with the bigquery.googleapis.com/storage/table_count metric and set a threshold alert
Enable BigQuery Admin resource charts, export metrics to Cloud Monitoring, and create an alerting policy on the slot utilization metric
Use Looker Studio to build a dashboard showing slot utilization with a 5-minute refresh

questions.explanation:
BigQuery exports slot utilization metrics (bigquery.googleapis.com/reservation/slot_utilization) to Cloud Monitoring. You can create alerting policies in Cloud Monitoring with a 5-minute alignment period and threshold condition. This is the native, low-latency, alerting-capable approach. INFORMATION_SCHEMA queries work for analysis but polling every minute via Cloud Functions adds unnecessary complexity.

[Professional Data Engineer exam guide](https://cloud.google.com/learn/certification/data-engineer)
questions.reference: Google Cloud Professional Data Engineer exam guide and product docs

questions.stem: A daily Dataproc job fails sporadically with 'YARN container killed due to exceeded memory' errors on worker nodes. The job runs identical code each day. What is the most likely cause?

options.text: Dataproc automatically scales down workers during the job, reducing available memory
Input data volume varies daily, causing memory pressure on days with larger files
The YARN container memory limit is too low; increase yarn.nodemanager.resource.memory-mb in cluster configuration
Dataproc does not support YARN resource management; switch to Kubernetes-based execution

questions.explanation:
Sporadic failures with identical code strongly suggest data volume variation rather than a configuration error. If the job succeeded historically with smaller inputs, the YARN limits were adequate then. The fix is to either increase worker memory, enable Dataproc autoscaling to add workers when memory pressure rises, or investigate and cap partition/file sizes upstream. A consistently failing job would indicate a configuration issue.

[Professional Data Engineer exam guide](https://cloud.google.com/learn/certification/data-engineer)
questions.reference: Google Cloud Professional Data Engineer exam guide and product docs

questions.stem: Your organization requires that all BigQuery query results be retained for 90 days for audit purposes. Queries run across multiple projects. What is the most scalable implementation?

options.text: Enable BigQuery audit logs to Cloud Logging and create a log sink to a Cloud Storage bucket with 90-day retention
Use BigQuery query history in the BigQuery UI, which retains 180 days of history automatically
Write a Cloud Functions trigger on every BigQuery job completion that copies results to a separate audit table
Enable INFORMATION_SCHEMA.JOBS_BY_ORGANIZATION export to a BigQuery audit dataset with a 90-day table expiration

questions.explanation:
Enabling Data Access audit logs (AUDIT_LOG_CONFIG for BigQuery) captures all queries organization-wide. A log sink to Cloud Storage with an object lifecycle policy for 90-day retention provides durable, scalable, low-cost audit storage. This works across all projects in the organization from a single org-level sink. INFORMATION_SCHEMA.JOBS only retains 180 days and is read-only from the UI; it's not an explicit retention mechanism. Cloud Functions per-job triggers are complex and error-prone at scale.

[Professional Data Engineer exam guide](https://cloud.google.com/learn/certification/data-engineer)
questions.reference: Google Cloud Professional Data Engineer exam guide and product docs

questions.stem: You are selecting a messaging service for a pipeline where exactly-once delivery semantics are critical and consumers need to replay messages from any offset. Which service best fits these requirements?

options.text: Cloud Pub/Sub standard topics
Cloud Pub/Sub with exactly-once delivery enabled
Cloud Pub/Sub with message ordering and a committed offset store
Managed Apache Kafka on Google Cloud

questions.explanation:
Apache Kafka provides native consumer group offsets stored in the broker, allowing consumers to seek to any offset and replay messages. Kafka's exactly-once semantics (with idempotent producers and transactional APIs) satisfies both requirements. Pub/Sub supports exactly-once delivery as of 2022 but does not natively support arbitrary offset replay — messages are delivered from the subscription's backlog, not from a persistent log with seekable offsets.

[Professional Data Engineer exam guide](https://cloud.google.com/learn/certification/data-engineer)
questions.reference: Google Cloud Professional Data Engineer exam guide and product docs

questions.stem: A Dataflow pipeline reading from Cloud Storage is processing files much slower than expected. The input is 50,000 small files (each ~10 KB). What is the primary performance issue?

options.text: Dataflow cannot process more than 10,000 files per job
Small file overhead: each file open/close incurs Cloud Storage API calls, creating too much overhead per record
The pipeline lacks sufficient workers; set --numWorkers=100
Cloud Storage does not support parallel reads from many small files

questions.explanation:
Processing many small files is a classic 'small files problem'. Each file requires a Cloud Storage API call for open and metadata, creating API overhead that dwarfs the actual data processing time. Best practice is to compact small files into larger files (e.g., 100–500 MB each) upstream, or use Cloud Storage Compose to merge them before processing. Adding workers helps throughput but does not reduce per-file API overhead.

[Professional Data Engineer exam guide](https://cloud.google.com/learn/certification/data-engineer)
questions.reference: Google Cloud Professional Data Engineer exam guide and product docs

questions.stem: You need to design a Cloud Bigtable row key for a table storing user click events. The access pattern is: retrieve all events for a specific user in a time range. Which row key design is best?

options.text: timestamp#user_id
user_id#timestamp
A UUID (random) as the row key
event_type#user_id#timestamp

questions.explanation:
Bigtable stores rows in lexicographic order. user_id#timestamp puts all events for a user in adjacent rows, enabling a range scan from user_id#start_time to user_id#end_time. timestamp#user_id groups all events at a given timestamp together (not useful for per-user queries) and causes write hotspots on the latest timestamp. Random UUIDs distribute writes evenly but cannot support range scans.

[Professional Data Engineer exam guide](https://cloud.google.com/learn/certification/data-engineer)
questions.reference: Google Cloud Professional Data Engineer exam guide and product docs

questions.stem: A data scientist needs to explore and clean a 50 GB CSV dataset before loading it into BigQuery. They prefer a visual, no-code interface. Which Google Cloud service is most appropriate?

options.text: Cloud Dataprep by Trifacta
Vertex AI Workbench (Jupyter notebooks)
BigQuery Data Transfer Service
Cloud Dataflow

questions.explanation:
Cloud Dataprep (powered by Trifacta/Alteryx) is Google Cloud's intelligent visual data preparation service. It provides a no-code UI for exploring, cleaning, and transforming data. It automatically detects schemas, suggests transformations, and profiles data quality. Dataflow and Jupyter require code. DTS is for ingestion, not exploration.

[Professional Data Engineer exam guide](https://cloud.google.com/learn/certification/data-engineer)
questions.reference: Google Cloud Professional Data Engineer exam guide and product docs

questions.stem: You need to implement CDC (Change Data Capture) from a Cloud SQL for PostgreSQL database into BigQuery with sub-minute latency. Which service provides this natively?

options.text: BigQuery Data Transfer Service with Cloud SQL connector
Datastream for BigQuery
Cloud Composer DAG polling Cloud SQL every minute
Database Migration Service with continuous replication

questions.explanation:
Datastream is Google Cloud's fully managed CDC and replication service. Datastream for BigQuery reads the PostgreSQL WAL (write-ahead log) and replicates INSERTs, UPDATEs, and DELETEs directly into BigQuery in near-real-time (typically sub-minute). It handles schema evolution and backfill automatically. DMS is for database migration (one-time or continuous to Cloud SQL/AlloyDB), not streaming to BigQuery. BQ DTS does not support CDC from Cloud SQL.

[Professional Data Engineer exam guide](https://cloud.google.com/learn/certification/data-engineer)
questions.reference: Google Cloud Professional Data Engineer exam guide and product docs

questions.stem: A BigQuery table stores orders. Analysts frequently query the last 90 days and filter by customer_region. The table has 5 years of data. Which combination of table features minimizes query cost?

options.text: Partition by order_date, cluster by customer_region
Cluster by order_date, partition by customer_region
Partition by customer_region, cluster by order_date
No partitioning; use materialized views aggregated by region

questions.explanation:
Partitioning by order_date allows BigQuery to skip all partitions outside the 90-day window, dramatically reducing bytes scanned. Clustering by customer_region then sorts data within each partition, reducing bytes scanned for region filters. This is the canonical pattern: partition on the time dimension (most selective for range queries), cluster on the most-filtered categorical column.

[Professional Data Engineer exam guide](https://cloud.google.com/learn/certification/data-engineer)
questions.reference: Google Cloud Professional Data Engineer exam guide and product docs

questions.stem: You need to share a BigQuery dataset with an external partner company. They should be able to query the data but cannot copy it to their own project. Which approach enforces this?

options.text: Grant the partner bigquery.dataViewer on the dataset and require their service account to sign a DUA
Use Analytics Hub to publish the dataset as a listing; the partner subscribes and gets a linked dataset they can query but not export
Create a Cloud Storage export nightly and share the bucket with the partner's project
Use VPC Service Controls to prevent the partner from copying data

questions.explanation:
Analytics Hub allows data publishers to share BigQuery datasets as listings. Subscribers get a linked dataset in their project — they can query the data but cannot copy tables, export to Storage, or run EXPORT DATA commands. This is the purpose-built GCP solution for governed cross-organization data sharing. Standard IAM grants alone do not prevent exporting data to another project.

[Professional Data Engineer exam guide](https://cloud.google.com/learn/certification/data-engineer)
questions.reference: Google Cloud Professional Data Engineer exam guide and product docs

questions.stem: Your team wants to implement data quality checks on BigQuery tables after each daily ETL pipeline run. Which Google Cloud service provides managed, declarative data quality rules for BigQuery without writing custom code?

options.text: Dataplex Auto Data Quality
Cloud Monitoring custom metrics
BigQuery scheduled queries with assertion checks
Cloud Data Catalog tag templates

questions.explanation:
Dataplex Auto Data Quality allows you to define declarative data quality rules (completeness, uniqueness, validity, custom SQL) on BigQuery tables and schedule them to run automatically. Results are stored in BigQuery and viewable in the Dataplex UI. This is the managed, no-code approach. Scheduled queries with assertions work but require custom SQL and result management. Monitoring metrics do not inspect data content.

[Professional Data Engineer exam guide](https://cloud.google.com/learn/certification/data-engineer)
questions.reference: Google Cloud Professional Data Engineer exam guide and product docs

questions.stem: You are building a real-time fraud detection system. Events must be scored within 200ms. The model is trained in Vertex AI. Which serving approach meets the latency requirement?

options.text: Batch prediction jobs run every hour, results stored in BigQuery for lookup
Vertex AI online prediction endpoint with the model deployed to an endpoint
Dataflow pipeline calling a model deployed on Vertex AI every 5 minutes
Export the model to Cloud Storage and load it in a Cloud Run service for inference

questions.explanation:
Vertex AI online prediction endpoints provide synchronous, low-latency (typically 10–100ms) model serving via REST/gRPC API. For real-time fraud scoring at <200ms, this is the standard pattern. Batch predictions run asynchronously and are not suitable for real-time scoring. Cloud Run with a loaded model is also valid and can meet the SLA, but Vertex AI endpoints provide managed scaling, monitoring, and A/B testing.

[Professional Data Engineer exam guide](https://cloud.google.com/learn/certification/data-engineer)
questions.reference: Google Cloud Professional Data Engineer exam guide and product docs

questions.stem: A Cloud Composer DAG orchestrates a multi-step pipeline. One task downloads data from an external API that is rate-limited to 10 requests per minute. The task exceeds the limit and fails. What is the best way to handle this in Airflow?

options.text: Set pool_slots=1 and create a pool with 10 slots for API tasks to limit concurrency
Use retries=3 with retry_delay=timedelta(minutes=1) to space out retries
Add a BashOperator sleep(60) task before the API task
Set max_active_runs=1 to prevent multiple DAG runs from executing simultaneously

questions.explanation:
Airflow Pools allow you to control resource consumption across tasks. Creating a pool (e.g., 'external_api_pool') with 10 slots and assigning all API tasks to it ensures no more than 10 concurrent requests are made, respecting the rate limit. This works across multiple DAG runs and tasks. Retries only handle failures after rate limiting occurs; they don't prevent the limit from being hit in the first place.

[Professional Data Engineer exam guide](https://cloud.google.com/learn/certification/data-engineer)
questions.reference: Google Cloud Professional Data Engineer exam guide and product docs

questions.stem: You need to detect PII (personally identifiable information) in unstructured text documents stored in Cloud Storage and redact it before loading into BigQuery. Which Google Cloud service automates this?

options.text: Cloud Data Loss Prevention (DLP) API with deidentification
Vertex AI Natural Language API entity extraction
BigQuery Data Masking with column-level security
Cloud KMS with envelope encryption of Cloud Storage objects

questions.explanation:
Cloud DLP (now Sensitive Data Protection) provides automated PII detection with 150+ built-in infoTypes (SSN, credit cards, email, phone numbers, etc.) and deidentification transformations (redaction, masking, tokenization, encryption). It can process Cloud Storage objects directly and write deidentified output. Vertex AI NL extracts named entities but does not redact them. Column-level security applies after loading, not before.

[Professional Data Engineer exam guide](https://cloud.google.com/learn/certification/data-engineer)
questions.reference: Google Cloud Professional Data Engineer exam guide and product docs

questions.stem: You need to enforce that BigQuery table data in the eu-west1 region never leaves the EU boundary. Which Google Cloud feature enforces this at the infrastructure level?

options.text: IAM conditions restricting access to EU IP ranges
VPC Service Controls perimeter around the BigQuery project
BigQuery dataset location set to EU with a VPC Service Controls service perimeter
Cloud Armor security policy blocking non-EU traffic

questions.explanation:
Setting the BigQuery dataset location to EU (or a specific EU region) ensures data storage does not leave EU infrastructure. VPC Service Controls adds an additional layer by creating a security perimeter that restricts which identities and networks can access the data, preventing exfiltration via API calls from outside the perimeter. Both controls are needed for comprehensive data residency enforcement.

[Professional Data Engineer exam guide](https://cloud.google.com/learn/certification/data-engineer)
questions.reference: Google Cloud Professional Data Engineer exam guide and product docs

questions.stem: A Dataflow job processes Pub/Sub messages but occasionally processes the same message twice, causing duplicate records in BigQuery. What should you implement to fix this?

options.text: Enable Dataflow exactly-once mode (--experiments=enable_exactly_once_delivery) and use BigQuery Storage Write API in committed mode
Increase the Pub/Sub subscription ack deadline to prevent re-delivery
Add a deduplication step using a GroupByKey on message_id before writing to BigQuery
Switch to Pub/Sub Lite which guarantees exactly-once delivery

questions.explanation:
Dataflow streaming exactly-once mode (available since Dataflow Runner V2) combined with BigQuery Storage Write API in COMMITTED mode provides end-to-end exactly-once processing. Dataflow guarantees exactly-once within the pipeline; the Storage Write API provides idempotent commits via stream offsets. GroupByKey deduplication adds latency and state overhead. Pub/Sub Lite does not guarantee exactly-once delivery.

[Professional Data Engineer exam guide](https://cloud.google.com/learn/certification/data-engineer)
questions.reference: Google Cloud Professional Data Engineer exam guide and product docs

questions.stem: Your organization wants to implement a governed data mesh architecture on Google Cloud. Each domain team should own and publish their data products independently. Which Google Cloud service best supports this architecture?

options.text: BigQuery authorized views shared via individual dataset IAM bindings
Dataplex for data governance and Analytics Hub for data product publishing
Cloud Data Catalog for metadata management across all domain datasets
A central Cloud Composer DAG orchestrating all domain data pipelines

questions.explanation:
A data mesh requires domain ownership (each team manages their data lake zone in Dataplex), data product publishing (Analytics Hub listings act as the data product marketplace), and centralized governance (Dataplex policies, data quality rules, and lineage). Dataplex + Analytics Hub is the GCP-native data mesh platform. A central Composer DAG contradicts domain ownership. Data Catalog provides discovery but not the full mesh architecture.

[Professional Data Engineer exam guide](https://cloud.google.com/learn/certification/data-engineer)
questions.reference: Google Cloud Professional Data Engineer exam guide and product docs

questions.stem: Which Apache Beam primitive is responsible for applying a user-defined function to each element in a PCollection?

options.text: GroupByKey
ParDo
Flatten
CoGroupByKey

questions.explanation:
ParDo (parallel do) is the fundamental element-wise transform in Apache Beam. It applies a DoFn to each element of a PCollection, optionally emitting zero or more output elements. GroupByKey groups elements by key. Flatten merges multiple PCollections. CoGroupByKey performs a relational join-like grouping across multiple keyed PCollections.

[Professional Data Engineer exam guide](https://cloud.google.com/learn/certification/data-engineer)
questions.reference: Google Cloud Professional Data Engineer exam guide and product docs

questions.stem: You need to implement automated rollback for a Dataflow streaming pipeline if error rate exceeds 5% within a 10-minute window after deployment. What is the recommended approach?

options.text: Use Cloud Monitoring alerting with a notification channel that triggers a Cloud Function to run the Dataflow job update API with the previous template version
Maintain a manual runbook and rely on on-call engineers to monitor and roll back if needed
Use Cloud Deploy with Dataflow Flex Templates and configure automated rollback targets
Set Dataflow's --maxNumWorkers=0 to pause processing when errors are detected

questions.explanation:
Cloud Monitoring can track Dataflow pipeline error rate metrics. An alerting policy with a 10-minute window and 5% error threshold triggers a Cloud Function via a Pub/Sub notification channel. The Cloud Function calls the Dataflow jobs.update API with the previous Flex Template version to roll back. Cloud Deploy does support Dataflow but automated rollback for streaming pipelines is currently not a built-in feature. This pattern is the recommended GCP approach.

[Professional Data Engineer exam guide](https://cloud.google.com/learn/certification/data-engineer)
